data.title: Netflix selloff seen as buying opportunity as analysts see growth, pricing power, and more M&A
data.description:
Netflix’s (NFLX) underwhelming guidance for the current quarter and full year took a heavy toll on shares late Thursday and into Friday’s premarket despite a 16% increase in revenue (its second-best quarter) and 50 basis point increase in its operating margin.

But with shares down another 10% premarket, sell-side analysts are calling this an opportune time to jump in and buy the streaming giant with “compelling pricing power, growth headroom, and dominant position in streaming” at an attractive price.

There were plenty of moving parts to consider in the company’s most recent quarterly results, not least of which was the subscription price hike and captured termination fee from the Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) deal. In addition, the decision by founder and former CEO Reed Hastings to not seek another term as board chairman also weighed on shares.

Making the case for a Buy rating despite disappointing guidance, Needham analyst Laura Martin advises clients to consider the company’s diversification into complementary businesses (voting, podcasts, video games, etc.), all of which will “grow engagement with NFLX’s IP, which should lower churn and increase pricing power over time."

J.P. Morgan also advises clients to take advantage of weakness in NFLX shares, as the results show “NFLX continues to execute well with considerable growth headroom.” Analyst Doug Anmuth points to the company’s primary internal quality engagement metric, which hit an all-time high thanks to content “punching above its weight in terms of retention.”

“The primary issue was overly optimistic expectations for US pricing and margin expectation, rather than any fundamental deterioration,” sums up Jefferies’ James Heaney.

As for the source of Netflix’s (NFLX) late afternoon/early morning sell-off, there’s little debate over how guidance was received, but there’s plenty of debate as to how these numbers may have been misinterpreted.

“We think [Q2 guide and lack of FY26 raise] are explained by the timing of U.S. price hikes, which typically take 2-3 months to filter through, while lapping last year’s increase and some conservatism early in the year,” says Morgan Stanley’s Sean Diffley.

Seeking Alpha analyst Luca Socci agrees that Netflix (NFLX) was being intentionally conservative.

“I think that analysts expected higher Q2 EPS because they likely assumed that the growing cash pile, thanks to the WBD fee, would have generated higher interest income. The fact that Netflix doesn’t embed this in its forecast either tells us that Netflix is being conservative or that its remaining buybacks are going to increase.”

Added Seeking Alpha analyst StockBros Research, “What I noticed is that investors ignored the 31.5% projected operating margin for the full year, which would be an improvement over 2025’s 29.5% margin. If Netflix achieves $51.2B in revenue with a 31.5% operating margin, then operating income will come out to $16.128B, or 21% higher year-over-year.”

While Netflix (NFLX) was unsuccessful in its pursuit of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), losing out to Paramount Skydance (PSKY), the company continues to prioritize growth through M&A—especially in artificial intelligence, as highlighted by its InterPositive acquisition.

“We have a unique capability to invest in generative AI to deliver returns to the business,” Co-CEO Gregory Peters said on the company’s earnings call with analysts.

“With our acquisition of InterPositive, we think it accelerates our GenAI capabilities because it's a proprietary technology that was created specifically for filmmakers and specifically for filmmaking, and that's different from other GenAI video applications,” co-CEO Ted Sarandos added on the call.

Taking the lessons from its WBD pursuit, the exercise not only showed Netflix management’s discipline in putting ego aside and walking away when the deal didn’t make sense financially but also “tested their investment discipline.”

“This experience underscores they do have the ability and willingness to do a large-scale deal, but the bar is extremely high and price matters,” said Morgan Stanley’s Diffley.

data.title: Stock index futures surge as Iran declares Strait of Hormuz open
data.description:
Stock index futures jumped after Iran said that the Strait of Hormuz was now open for commercial vessels. 

Dow futures (INDU) rose 1.1%, while S&P 500 futures (SPX) edged up 0.8% and Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) were up 0.9%. 

The Strait of Hormuz is now "completely open" for all commercial vessels for the rest of the ceasefire timeline, Iran said.

Sentiment had improved after a 10-day ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel took effect, with Trump expressing confidence that an agreement could soon be reached to end the Iran conflict and urging the Tehran-aligned Hezbollah group to hold its fire.

U.S. Treasury yields edged lower across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 1.3 basis points to 4.31%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) slipped 0.4 basis points to 3.78%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) declined 1 basis point to 4.93%.

Investors were preparing for a busy schedule of Federal Reserve commentary, with speeches expected from San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly, Richmond Fed President Thomas Barkin, and Fed Governor Christopher Waller.

data.title: Critical Metals surges after boosting stake in Greenland rare earths asset to 92.5%
data.description:
Critical Metals (CRML) up 22.4% pre-market Friday after saying Greenland's government approved the transfer to the company of the remaining 50.5% interest in the Tanbreez rare earth deposit, bringing the company's total ownership of the world's largest rare earth deposits to 92.5%.

The move gives Critical Metals (CRML) majority control of the deposit, which contains rare earths such as terbium and dysprosium used in consumer electronics and defense technology.

The transfer approval comes as Western governments and industry are seeking secure, non-Chinese sources of rare earth supply; with near-total ownership of Tanbreez, Critical Metals (CRML) said it is now uniquely positioned to become a cornerstone supplier of heavy rare earth elements.

"This is a game-changing moment for Critical Metals Corp. and for Western rare earth supply security, [which] removes the most significant structural overhang on the project and provides the clarity to advance Tanbreez to production with confidence," Critical Metals (CRML) Chairman Tony Sage said.

data.title: What bubble? AI startups score $242B in venture funding last quarter
data.description:
The artificial intelligence industry continues to draw billions of dollars in funding, with investors shrugging off fears of a potential bubble and doubling down on the technology that promises to improve productivity and efficiency.

Investors poured $300B into 6,000 startups globally in the first quarter of 2026, up over 150% Q/Q and Y/Y, Crunchbase data showed. And most of these investments were disproportionately in AI startups, particularly in the U.S.

Around $242B, or 80% of the total global venture funding in Q1, went to AI companies. The previous record was set in Q1 2025, when AI accounted for 55% of global venture funding, according to Crunchbase.

The largest funding round on record closed in Q1 2026 – Microsoft (MSFT)-backed OpenAI (OPENAI) raised a whopping $122B at an $852B valuation.

Anthropic (ANTHRO), which scored heavy investments from Amazon (AMZN) and Google (GOOGL), secured $30B in its last funding round. 

xAI (X.AI), which is now part of SpaceX (SPACE), raised $20B earlier this year. xAI counts Nvidia (NVDA) and Cisco (CSCO) as its strategic minority partners.

Notably, OpenAI (OPENAI), Anthropic (ANTHRO) and xAI (X.AI) are all scaling aggressively while remaining unprofitable. And they are each targeting to go public soon, potentially this year.

But geopolitical tensions are casting a shadow over venture capital globally on account of rising oil prices, renewed inflation fears and possible interest rate hikes.

"While this could keep IPO activity subdued in the near term, AI is expected to remain a clear standout for investors globally, alongside defense tech, space tech, and cybersecurity," said Carolina de Oliveira, global lead of Emerging Giants, KPMG International.

data.title: Thomasville Bancshares GAAP EPS of $1.86
data.description:
• Thomasville Bancshares press release (THVB): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.86.
  • Pre-tax pre-provision income of $16.9 million, an increase of $2.4 million, or 16%, compared to the same period in 2025.
  • Return on Average Assets of 2.44% and Return on Average Tangible Equity of 24.66%.
  • Total Assets of $2.095 billion, an increase of $196 million over the same period in 2025.
  • Loans grew $196 million to $1.788 billion, or 12% year-over-year.
  • Deposits increased $148 million to $1.790 billion, or 9% year-over-year.
  • Regulatory Capital was $209 million or 10.14% of assets.
  • TNB Financial, provider of trust and investment services, has client assets over $5.4 billion.

data.title: Nauticus Robotics to implement 1-for-8 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Nauticus Robotics (KITT) on Friday said it will proceed with a 1-for-8 reverse stock split of its common stock following approval from its board and shareholders.
  • The company said the reverse split is expected to take effect on April 21, with trading on a post-split basis to begin the same day under the ticker "KITT".
  • Nauticus said the move is aimed at increasing its share price to meet Nasdaq’s minimum bid price requirement.
  • KITT -19.48% premarket to $0.409.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Fuerte Metals Corp. GAAP EPS of -C$0.13
data.description:
• Fuerte Metals Corp. press release (FMT:CA):
  • FY GAAP EPS of -C$0.13.
  • As of December 31, 2025, cash stood at C$56.86M.

data.title: Alstom plunges 30% after pulling cash flow guidance
data.description:
Alstom (ALSMY) (AOMFF) sank nearly 30% in European trading Friday, on course for its worst single-day decline since October 2023, after withdrawing its financial guidance, citing slow progress on key projects for railway rolling stock.

The French train maker pulled its target for €1.5B (~$1.77B) in cumulative free cash flow for the three years until ​the end of the current 2026-27 financial year, as well as its target of an 8%-10% adjusted EBIT margin by FY 2027.

Alstom (ALSMY) (AOMFF) issued preliminary results for FY 2026 ended March 31, saying its adjusted EBIT margin fell to 6%, down from 6.4% in the previous fiscal year and lower than the 7% it previously forecast, and free cash flow declined to €330M from €502M in FY 2025.

Orders for the fiscal year jumped 42% Y/Y on an organic basis to €27.6B, while sales grew 7% organically to €19.2B.

"In a business where rigorous planning and disciplined execution are essential, some large rolling-stock projects have progressed more slowly than anticipated, weighing on near-term margins and cash," new CEO Martin Sion said, without describing which projects and regions were hit by the delays.

"We are therefore launching immediate actions to stabilize performance, while preparing deeper operational changes to restore sustainable execution, cash generation, and profitable growth," Sion said.

"The core issue is engineering execution rather than demand, with delays in homologation, German ramp-ups, and late‑stage deliveries driving cost overruns," Redburn analyst James Moore said in a note.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: DIS, XOM, AAPL, FITB
data.description:
Stock index futures edged higher on Tuesday. Here are the four stocks to watch on the day:

Disney (DIS) rose 0.08% in premarket trade as the media giant unveiled plans for Super Bowl LXI in 2027. Under its rights deal with the NFL, the game will be telecast on both ABC and ESPN, with a separate “alterna-cast” led by Peyton and Eli Manning to appear on ESPN2. The Super Bowl is set to take place on Valentine’s Day and will be followed by a federal holiday, potentially amplifying the event’s spectacle.

Exxon Mobil (XOM) fell 1.21% before the opening bell after withdrawing an offer to sell two initial cargoes of liquefied natural gas from its Golden Pass export plant in Texas, according to Reuters. The plant has been running at roughly a third of its capacity since starting production last month, taking in ~287 million cubic feet per day of natural gas on Thursday—well below its first train’s nameplate capacity of 800 million cubic feet per day.

Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB) slipped 0.10% in premarket trading after loan loss provisions came in higher than estimated, weighing on first-quarter results. The bank reported Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.15, missing the S&P Capital IQ consensus estimate of $0.22, while revenue of $2.83 billion trailed expectations by $9 million. The company noted a net negative $0.68 impact from certain items.

Apple (AAPL) gained in premarket trade following data showing iPhone shipments rose 20% in China during the first quarter. According to preliminary figures from Counterpoint Research’s Market Monitor Tracker, the growth was driven by strong performance of the iPhone 17 series, promotional price cuts, and government subsidies. Apple ranked second in China’s smartphone market with a 19% share, just behind Huawei at 20%.

data.title: SunOpta shareholders approve buyout deal
data.description:
SunOpta (STKL) announced that its shareholders approved at a special meeting the acquisition of the company by Refresco at a deal price of $6.50 per share.  The deal values SunOpta (STKL) at $1.1B on an enterprise value basis 

The merger was approved by 98.1% of the votes cast at the special meeting, and a non-binding, advisory executive compensation proposal was approved by 82.5% of the votes cast at the special meeting.

The closing of the deal is still subject to remaining regulatory clearance or approval, approval by the Ontario Superior Court of Justice, and the satisfaction or waiver of other customary closing conditions. 

Upon completion of the transaction, SunOpta (STKL) will become a wholly owned subsidiary of Refresco. In light of the pending transaction, SunOpta (STKL) suspended its quarterly earnings conference calls and will no longer be providing quarterly or annual guidance.

SunOpta (STKL) CEO Brian Kocher said the strategic combination validates the vision of transforming SunOpta (STKL) into a premier solutions partner in the high-growth better-for-you food and beverage space. "Over the past several years, we've built exceptional platforms serving marquee customers and consistently delivering double-digit growth while maintaining the highest food safety and quality standards. This partnership with Refresco provides the resources and scale to unlock SunOpta's full potential," he added.
